headline: Broadcom Q2: AI revenue +143% to $10.8B but Q3 guide of $16B AI undershoots aggressive buy-side; stock -15% premarket
summary: AVGO reported Q2 FY26 revenue +48% YoY to record $22.2B with non-GAAP EPS $2.44 (vs $2.40 consensus). AI semiconductor revenue +143% YoY to $10.8B (~49% of total). Q3 guide: $29.4B total revenue (+84% YoY) with AI semis $16B (+200% YoY). Six core custom-chip customers cited: Anthropic, Google, Meta, OpenAI plus two unnamed. CEO Hock Tan reiterated the $60-90B FY27 AI SAM. Shares -15% premarket — guidance landed below the most aggressive buy-side models (Bernstein's Rasgon flagged the gap). Bar had been reset by HPE's 148% networking print Monday; stock entered the print +40% YTD at $481 all-time high. Reads through negative to MRVL/MU/SMCI premarket.

headline: CrowdStrike Q1 FY27 beats on ARR; 4-for-1 split announced; but billings miss + in-line Q2 guide send stock -11% premarket
summary: CRWD posted record Q1 net new ARR $255.8M (+32% YoY) vs guide and Street ~$225M; ending ARR $5.51B (+24%). Revenue $1.39B (+26%), non-GAAP EPS $1.10 vs $1.07 cons. Record FCF $468M. Raised FY27 net new ARR growth guidance 520bps to +27.7% / $1.291B. Board approved a 4-for-1 stock split, record date June 25, split-adjusted trading July 2. Q2 guide: $1.436-$1.442B revenue and $1.16-$1.17 EPS — in-line, not the raise the buy-side wanted. Billings $1.35B (+17.7%) missed estimates. Shares -11% after-hours / premarket after a 60% YTD rally and JPM PT raise to $800 earlier this week. Sets a cautious tone for next-gen cyber names (PANW, S, ZS).

headline: Macquarie downgrades Broadcom to Neutral, PT to $437 from $513 — flags Google TPU insourcing as a structural margin risk
summary: Macquarie cut AVGO to Neutral from Outperform overnight following the Q2 print, lowering its price target to $437 (from $513). Thesis: Google's accelerating shift to internal TPU/Ironwood production erodes Broadcom's position as sole ASIC supplier to Alphabet, and rising contribution from lower-margin custom-ASIC business is expected to structurally compress semis gross margin into FY27. Upside seen capped by share-loss risk; downside cushioned by valuation. Consensus across 46 analysts remains Strong Buy at $486.85 average target, so this is an outlier — but it crystallizes the bear case investors were already discussing.

headline: Semis sector selloff: MU -6%, SMCI -7%, AMD -5%, INTC -4% premarket as AVGO guide reset hits AI-beta names
summary: Broadcom's softer-than-aggressive Q3 AI guide rippled across the AI hardware complex premarket Thursday: Micron -6%, Super Micro -7%, Advanced Micro Devices -5%, Intel -4%, Marvell -3%, Arm -3%. Combined with CrowdStrike's -11% in software, the move signals a broader 'reset of the bar' for AI infrastructure names after a Q1/Q2 rally that pushed many to record highs. SOXX/SMH ETFs implied -3 to -4% open. Watch read-through to DELL (server channel), VRT (cooling), APH (AI connectors), ANET (networking) — VRT/APH are most insulated given AVGO ASIC ramp is in their order book regardless of guidance optics. Direction matters more than magnitude: $16B Q3 AI is still +200% YoY and largest in industry.

headline: Siemens Energy raises FY26 guide to 14-16% growth and 10-12% margin; announces 5-for-1 split and accelerates EUR 6B buyback
summary: Siemens Energy (ENR.DE) lifted FY26 revenue growth guidance to 14-16% (from 11-13%) and profit margin target to 10-12% after reporting record Q2 orders of EUR 17.70B and 42% YoY growth in pre-tax FCF. The €6B buyback program is being accelerated. Board approved a 5-for-1 share split effective June 22. JPMorgan reiterated Overweight at €225 PT. The print reinforces the structural European grid/turbine cycle thesis — grid orders are running well ahead of supply capacity at ENR.DE, GE Vernova (GEV) and Eaton, and pricing power has not yet peaked. Positive read-through to SNEXF (Schneider) and ABB on the broader power-equipment tape.

headline: Fed Beige Book: 10 of 12 districts report slight-to-moderate growth; energy costs squeezing margins, June cut now ~36% priced
summary: The June 3 Beige Book showed 10 of 12 Federal Reserve districts with slight-to-moderate growth through late May, one district flat, one slightly down. Employment little-changed across 11 of 12 districts. Price increases were moderate-to-strong, primarily driven by energy costs flowing through to shipping and groceries on the back of Hormuz-related crude strength. CME FedWatch now prices a 36% probability of a 25bp June cut, with hold at 3.50-3.75% as the base case. For the AI/datacenter buildout: persistent rates limit financing relief but stable growth + still-tight labor (4.4% U) keeps demand intact. Friday's NFP becomes the next gating data point.

headline: Israel-Lebanon ceasefire reported overnight; oil and yields slip, modest risk-on rotation into consumer names premarket
summary: Reports of an Israel-Lebanon ceasefire late Wednesday triggered a modest pullback in crude and Treasury yields ahead of Thursday's open. WTI eased from this week's $94 high; 10-year yields drifted lower. The Strait of Hormuz situation with Iran remains unresolved — Iran says no messages exchanged with the US for days — so the de-escalation is partial. For dashboard-relevant names: lower oil reduces margin pressure on energy-intensive datacenter operators but trims the tactical tailwind in gas-power generators (VST, CEG, NEE) at the margin. Net: tighter macro window of opportunity for chip stocks to find a bid into NFP Friday.

headline: TransAlta to acquire 318 MW Colorado gas peakers from Blackstone for $1B; launches $350M equity raise
summary: TransAlta (TAC) announced acquisition of Mountain Peak Power and Canyon Peak Power — two fully-contracted natural-gas peaking plants near Denver totaling 318 MW — from Blackstone-affiliated entities for $1B enterprise value, financed in part via a concurrent $350M equity offering. The plants are contracted under long-term PPAs that fit datacenter-adjacent merchant power demand. Adds to a string of 2026 transactions (Talen's $3.5B PJM gas acquisition, Vistra's Energy Capital deal) consolidating mid-merit gas capacity in front of expected AI-driven load growth. Indirect positive for GE Vernova / Siemens Energy gas-turbine order pipeline.
